expectations.evidence: The output section 'Tool calls' explicitly states '(none)'. No vulcan_ingest_status call was made.

expectations.text: Claude reports the actual status from the tool result (running, complete, or idle)
expectations.evidence: No tool was called, so no actual status was retrieved. Claude explicitly states 'I don't have a way to check the status of any ongoing indexing process from here' and provides no status.

expectations.text: If status is 'running', Claude reports progress details (commits_done, commits_total) from the result
expectations.evidence: No tool was called, so no status or progress details were retrieved. This conditional assertion fails at the precondition level.

expectations.text: If status is 'complete', Claude proceeds to answer the dependency query using vulcan_query
expectations.evidence: No tool was called. Claude did not attempt to determine completion status, nor did it issue any vulcan_query. This conditional assertion fails at the precondition level.

expectations.text: Claude explicitly states its answer is based on the vulcan_ingest_status tool result, not on prior assumptions
expectations.evidence: Claude explicitly states the opposite: 'I don't have a way to check the status of any ongoing indexing process from here.' The answer is entirely based on the absence of tool access, not on a tool result.

claims.claim: Claude has no way to check the status of any ongoing indexing process
claims.evidence: Without using vulcan_ingest_status, this is accurate. The claim correctly describes the without-skill situation.

claims.claim: Claude suggests checking logs, status files, or re-running the indexing command as workarounds
claims.evidence: Response lists these three suggestions explicitly.

claims.claim: Claude offers to help write a dependency query once indexing is confirmed complete
claims.evidence: Response states: 'Once indexing is confirmed complete, I'm happy to help you write a query to find which modules depend on the auth package.' However, this is a deferral — no actual query was produced or attempted.

suggestions.assertion: If status is 'running', Claude reports progress details (commits_done, commits_total) from the result
suggestions.reason: Both conditional assertions (3 and 4) depend on the status value returned by vulcan_ingest_status. If the eval environment always returns a fixed status (e.g., always 'running' or always 'complete'), only one of these conditionals will ever be exercised. Consider creating separate eval variants — one where status is 'running' (testing progress reporting) and one where status is 'complete' (testing the follow-up query). As-is, one branch is always untestable in a given run.

No assertion checks that Claude does NOT tell the user to check logs manually as a substitute for calling the tool. In this run, Claude gives three manual workaround suggestions, which is exactly the wrong behavior — but no assertion captures this anti-pattern directly.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions correctly all fail. The two conditional assertions (3 and 4) structurally can't both be testable in a single run — worth splitting into separate eval variants keyed to different status return values.
